paragraphs.body: The Great Value of an Obstacle

Not one person is perfect. That is the lesson that many people learn as they grow up. People make mistakes and they can be life altering, but the most important part is to grow and get better from it. One of my biggest icons when I was little was Juventus, an Italian soccer team that is based in Turin, so when I discovered that one of my icons were punished because of a cheating scandal, I started to doubt myself. That was a team I looked up to and wanted to play with great honor. I thought to myself how a great team cheat and get punished, but still be one of the most known teams in Europe in recent history. I realized something great that someone could fail and be brought down, but if you put your mind to it and work hard, they can accomplish great things and be brought back to glory.

paragraphs.body: Juventus has always been my favorite team since I could remember. Our father and our grandfather have always praised and cheered them on too. I have looked up to them and cheered them on with my family for a long time, so when our dad taught me that the team almost failed and dismantled, I was surprised because of how successful they are now. Juventus had just won the 2004 and 2005 Italian championships. They were on top of Italian soccer, even getting close to Winning the Champions League trophy- which is the most prestigious trophy in Europe in a soccer season. In the summer of 2006 this all changed. There was investigation on Juventus because the Italian federation of soccer thought the Juventus players were taking performance enhanced drugs, because of their performance in the last two years. The investigation came up empty handed, until a journalist got an anonymous hint that Juventus and many other teams were blackmailing and threatening the referees to give them better chances during the game. This was a very bad look on Juventus because of their recent success, so a full-on investigation was on its way. All in all, Juventus was in big trouble and were found guilty on many charges. The punishment was that they were being relegated to the second tier of Italian soccer and that they had to forfeit their two Italian titles that they had just won the years past. Many Journalists thought that this was a very big deal in Italy. A scandal of this proportion was never seen in Italian soccer. In the view of Aditya Chauhan’s article, “It was the scandal that rocked Italian football.” The essence of Chauhan’s argument is to show the great seriousness of this scandal. Soccer in Italy is a very big deal, so the shamefulness and misery that the team had to deal with was an awful feeling. According to Aditya Chauhan the author of “Calciopoli Scandal: Why Juventus were relegated in 2006?”, “The aftermath of Calciopoli… leaving the disgraced institution of Italian football in complete and utter disarray.” In making this comment, Chauhan claims that after Juventus was caught and charged of breaking the financial law, they became a disgrace to Italian football. Although they became the disgrace of Italian football, it did not matter because they built there program up and built an amazing franchise. During the 2010’s they won 9 Italian championships in a row. They still perform and are ranked very highly today. This proves that things can be put in your way, but the important part is to never give up and build from the mistakes you make.

annotations.text: This proves that things can be put in your way, but the important part is to never give up and build from the mistakes you make.

paragraphs.body: This reminds me of a very similar situation. You know that I have always loved sports, especially soccer. Although I have a love for sports, my injury problems always get in the way of playing. I played two years of high school soccer, and I was had a great time, but I had many issues in staying healthy. You remember sophomore year; I got injured in summer camp right before the season. It was a right hamstring injury, and I couldn’t walk on it, and you had to do all the chores for a week until it got better. But I got better and played almost every game in the season. Then there was Junior year where you were at the game, and I went for a fifty-fifty challenge and broke my ankle. I was so tired at that point of getting injured that I wanted to quit, but I did not quit because I worked too hard for it. The year after that, my senior year. The biggest year of high school soccer. The year where it was supposed to be the most fun and most challenging year, and I broke my foot even before the first game and was out for the whole season. Although I was very sad, I worked my hardest to get healthy and to get back to where I was before my injury. Now because I did not quit then I have the fortune to play intramurals soccer at college with my friends every weekend. I always told you when we were little to not give up and this is one of my best examples of showing that. When you have a passion for something, you shouldn’t let an obstacle get in your way to stop you from achieving your goals. You must be like Juventus. If something brings you down and get dumped to the bottom, you fight your way up until you happy and believing in the things you like.

paragraphs.body: Just like you. You started dancing when you were just five years old. You barely knew how to speak English, since we just moved her from Italy. You did not care to be the best; you were just excited that you were dancing and you enjoying your time. That’s how you must think about an obstacle because if you find something that you enjoy you want to do it. Now yes as you get older it becomes more competitive and it might not get fun any longer, but when you must find a passion, so the sacrifices you made are worth it. Even though you get hurt or something doesn’t go your way, it does not matter you don’t quit.

paragraphs.body: This is a lesson you will learn in your future. Finding the best out of something unenjoyable is a great skill that should be valued. So, continue to dance. Your future will be impacted in a great way, and you can learn so much.
